summary: "Import from Portfolio" button performs no portfolio import — it silently opens the same blank manual-entry form as "Add Manually", despite a real GitHub/portfolio-scraper capability existing in the backend that is never wired to it.
reproduction: Log in as admin/admin123, go to /dashboard/stories.
Append ?demo=empty to the URL to view the empty state (client-side only; does not delete real data): https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/dashboard/stories?demo=empty
Click the 'Import from Portfolio' button (data-testid=empty-import-portfolio).
Observe: no network request to any portfolio/GitHub endpoint fires; the app simply opens the identical blank 'NEW STORY' manual form and silently exits the demo-empty view.

Compare with app/services/portfolio_scraper.py (scrape_github_profile) and career_data.py, which implement a real GitHub/portfolio ingestion pipeline used elsewhere (Settings > Career Data) but never called from Story Bank.
expected: Per the wireframe (design/screens/story-bank.html, data-design-id=empty-import-portfolio-sb25) and the empty-state copy ("Import achievements from your resume and portfolio...Aether will auto-extract STAR+R stories"), clicking Import from Portfolio should trigger a distinct import pipeline sourced from the user's portfolio/GitHub data — not the manual entry form.
observed: Click handler for empty-import-portfolio is wired to the same openCreate() used by 'Add Manually' (apps/web/src/app/dashboard/stories/page.tsx, empty-state buttons). Zero API calls fire. The button's label promises functionality that does not exist.

summary: An unbroken long string (no whitespace) typed into any STAR field breaks the ENTIRE page layout — the document balloons to 130,000-180,000px wide because story-card paragraphs have no overflow-wrap/break-words CSS, forcing horizontal scroll across the whole app shell (sidebar, header, aside included).
reproduction: Log in as admin/admin123, go to /dashboard/stories, click New Story.
Set Situation to a 15,000-20,000 character string with NO spaces (e.g. 'A'.repeat(20000)); fill Title/Task/Action/Result with short text; submit.
Story is created (201) and rendered on the card.
Run: document.documentElement.scrollWidth in devtools/Playwright.
Observed scrollWidth: 180302px on first run, 135302px on second (fresh-session) run with a 15,000-char string — the whole page, not just the card, gains a massive horizontal scrollbar.
expected: Long unbroken text should wrap within its card column (or be visually truncated/scroll-contained inside the card), never inflate the page's overall document width.
observed: document.documentElement.scrollWidth reached 180302px (run 1) and 135302px (run 2, fresh session) after submitting an unbroken long string; verified via cropped screenshot showing the situation text running off-card to the right for its full length.

summary: Deleting a story is immediate and irreversible with NO confirmation dialog — a single accidental click on the trash icon permanently destroys a STAR narrative with no undo.
reproduction: Log in, go to /dashboard/stories.
Click the trash-can icon on any story card.

Observe: no browser confirm()/modal dialog appears (verified via a page.on('dialog') listener that never fired); the DELETE /stories/{id} call fires immediately and the card is removed from the DOM and, after reload, from the server.
expected: A destructive, non-recoverable action on user-authored content (career achievement narratives) should require an explicit confirmation step before the DELETE call fires.
observed: No confirmation UI of any kind; delete-story-btn's onClick calls onDelete directly (apps/web/src/components/stories/story-card.tsx / page.tsx `remove`), which awaits DELETE and immediately filters local state. Reproduced twice across two fresh sessions with zero dialogs observed.

summary: DELETE /stories/{id} calls are reported by the browser network stack as net::ERR_ABORTED on every delete performed in this session (4/4 across two fresh sessions), even though the deletion completes correctly server-side every time (confirmed via reload + direct GET /stories cross-check). Harmless to the user, but pollutes error-rate/network telemetry with false failure signals.
reproduction: Log in, go to /dashboard/stories, delete any story while a network listener captures page.on('requestfailed').
Observe a requestfailed event for the DELETE request with failure text net::ERR_ABORTED.
Independently GET /stories (fresh curl with the session token) and confirm the story id is actually gone — deletion succeeded despite the reported network-level abort.
expected: A successful DELETE (204) should not surface as a browser-level aborted/failed request.
observed: 4/4 delete operations across two independent sessions logged net::ERR_ABORTED on the DELETE XHR while the underlying deletion was verified successful both times via direct API re-check.

summary: Production's unrotated /var/log/aether/api.log retains historical, un-timestamped log lines proving the pre-fix GAP-P6-AUTH-002 defect (silently serving a static test fixture as if it were live LLM output) DID occur for the storyExtractor agent (5x) and for tailor/coverLetter (52x) in this environment — the exact 'fabricated content presented as real' behavior CLM-024's '27/27 gates VERIFIED-CLOSED' claims is closed. Fresh live re-verification in THIS session (2x storyExtractor runs, most recent 2026-07-17T16:01:49Z) shows current behavior is honest and real, and no currently-visible Story Bank content matches the known fixture fingerprints — so this is very likely a historical/pre-deploy artifact rather than a live-reproducing defect, but it cannot be dated precisely because the log carries no timestamps and is never rotated.
reproduction: On the production host, run: grep -n "served fixture fallback" /var/log/aether/api.log — returns 57 matches (5 for prompt='story_extractor', e.g. lines 11804/11818/12272/12630/15396 in the snapshot taken at 2026-07-17T15:58Z).
Search the entire current source tree for that exact string: grep -rn "served fixture fallback" apps/ — zero matches; the string does not exist anywhere in current code.

git log -p -- apps/api/app/services/llm_client.py shows commit 0f7a5ff (2026-07-16T12:33:01Z, 'fix(GAP-P6-AUTH-002...): honest LLM failure (no fixture masquerade)') DELETED the exact code path that logged this line and replaced it with an honest LLMUnavailableError (no fixture served) — confirmed present in the current file at apps/api/app/services/llm_client.py:1057-1065.

Trigger a fresh storyExtractor run right now (POST /agents/story-extractor/run) and grep the newly appended log lines: clean, real live call (model qwen/qwen3-coder-next), no 'served fixture fallback' text — reproduced twice (UI-triggered run_id cfb8f5408c4a09f58992b803a at 15:50:48Z, and direct API run_id c974b24738b44a2793ee9b0d2 at 16:01:49Z).
expected: Per CLM-024 and the GAP-P6-AUTH-002 fix commit, the platform should NEVER have silently served fixture content as live LLM output; log evidence of this happening for storyExtractor should not exist in a system whose gates were genuinely 'VERIFIED-CLOSED'.
observed: The historical log evidence exists (57 occurrences platform-wide, 5 for story_extractor) and does not correspond to any code currently on disk. Current live behavior (verified twice, freshest evidence 2026-07-17T16:01:49Z) is honest and healthy. No fixture-fingerprint titles are present in the live Story Bank for this account.
evidence: Direct production log inspection at 2026-07-17T15:58-16:06Z (not file-copied per no-secrets/no-bulk-log rule; line numbers and exact log text captured above are reproducible via the commands listed in `reproduction`)

summary: storyExtractor always re-parses ONE fixed, backend-configured resume PDF (AETHER_RESUME_PDF env var, defaulting to assets/resume/Vik_Resume_Final.pdf) — it ignores any specific resume version a user has uploaded/selected in Resume Studio, and there is no way to supply free-text source content anywhere in the Story Bank UI. All three trigger buttons (Import from Resume, Draft missing stories, and the auto-trigger on resume upload) call the same parameterless POST /agents/story-extractor/run.
reproduction: Read apps/api/app/agents/story_extractor.py: run(self, user_id) calls parse_resume_pdf(get_base_resume_path())["raw_text"] — get_base_resume_path() (apps/api/app/agents/fit_scorer.py:24-25) takes no user_id/resume_id argument at all.
Read apps/web/src/app/dashboard/stories/page.tsx and story-aside.tsx: extract() calls runStoryExtractor() with no body; lib/api/stories.ts confirms the POST body is empty.

Read apps/api/app/routers/resumes.py:upload_resume — even the auto-trigger fired after a resume upload calls _dispatch(user_id, 'storyExtractor', {}), which still reads the fixed PDF, not the just-uploaded raw_text.
expected: The wireframe's 'Import from Resume' / empty-state copy ('Import achievements from your resume') implies extraction is grounded in the user's own (current/selected) resume content.
observed: Extraction is grounded in a single, fixed, operator-configured PDF regardless of which user or resume version triggers it. For the admin/Vikram account under test this happens to be correct (the fixed PDF is Vikram's real resume, so output is genuinely grounded and non-fabricated — see MV-story-bank agent-run findings), but the mechanism is not user/resume-scoped, which could produce a different user's resume content in a different account's Story Bank.

summary: Stat strip labels differ from the wireframe: wireframe shows 'Used This Month' and 'Voice Match Avg' (both with hardcoded demo numbers, 11 and 94%); the live app shows 'Starred' and 'Categories Covered' instead, computed from real data.
reproduction: Compare design/screens/story-bank.html section data-design-id=story-stats-sb06 (4 tiles: Total Stories, Quantified w/ Metrics, Used This Month, Voice Match Avg) against the live stat strip at /dashboard/stories (data-testid=story-stats).
expected: Wireframe conformance would show 'Used This Month' and 'Voice Match Avg' tiles.
observed: apps/api/app/routers/stories.py's module docstring explains this is a deliberate anti-fabrication decision: 'No usage or voice metrics are exposed — nothing tracks them yet, and invented numbers must never be presented as real.' The substituted tiles (Starred, Categories Covered) are both real, live-computed values. This is a justified, documented deviation, not a defect, but is logged per protocol §3.2.1 (every visible deviation = finding).
